rag_rules: When AGREE validator reports syntax errors like '<EOF>' or type errors, trace the root cause to unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations in the same annex block; fix or remove the problematic clause instead of adding new architecture.
Ensure that all guarantee expressions reference only visible AADL features or helper declarations defined within the same annex block.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, place assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes when they refer to component ports.
Use only named constants, visible feature names, or concrete bounds in type-level assumptions and guarantees.
For output envelope guarantees, ensure bounded outputs are constrained within valid min/max ranges and that clamping behavior is explicitly stated.
When a guarantee references an identifier with type <error>, trace the first unresolved symbol on the same line and resolve or remove the clause.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must end with a semicolon after each clause; missing semicolons may cause '<EOF>' errors.
Guarantee expressions must reference only visible identifiers or helper declarations defined in the same annex block.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to add new architecture or components to resolve AGREE type or syntax errors; instead, fix or remove the invalid clause.
Avoid referencing subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
